name: Lemony Bulgur and Chickpea Pilaf
recipeIngredient: 1 teaspoon ground cumin, or more to taste
2 cups cooked chickpeas (1 cup dried) OR 1 14-oz can, drained
salt and freshly ground pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the bulgur in a bowl. Bring the stock to a boil, add half the cumin, and pour the stock over the bulgur. Stir once and let sit for 10-15 minutes until most of the liquid has been absorbed and the bulgur is fluffy.
2. Heat the olive oil in a large heavy-bottomed skillet (or Dutch oven) over medium heat. Add the onion, green pepper, and half the garlic. Sauté, stirring, until the onion is translucent, about 3 to 5 minutes.
3. Add the remaining garlic and cumin. Sauté for about 30 seconds.
4. Stir in the bulgur and chickpeas. Combine briefly.
5. Add the lemon juice and parsley. Mix well, add salt and pepper, and remove from heat. Serve hot.
Notes:
- Vegetable bouillon cube was more than salty enough.
- A pinch of cayenne might make it even better.
